task: I'm seriously thinking about applying to MBA programs in the U.S., and I want a solid research sheet I can actually use to decide where to apply and where funding might be strongest. Please start by using U.S. News and then Poets&Quants to build a defensible list of the top 20 full-time MBA programs in the United States, mainly so I have a realistic shortlist based on major rankings rather than just reputation. Once that list looks settled, go to each school's official MBA admissions site and open every program's admissions page in its own tab so I can visually inspect the official pages later, and for each one capture the application deadline, the GMAT/GRE or test-waiver policy, the application fee, the program length, and the admissions URL. After that, use the official business school or university financial aid pages for those same schools to find MBA-specific fellowships, scholarships, or named funding programs, and whenever you find one, open the actual official funding page in its own tab and verify the eligibility rules and the funding amount if the page lists one. I want at least 20 schools and at least 20 total funding opportunities across them, so if a school's main admissions page is vague, keep digging on the official school domain until you find the clearest scholarship or fellowship source. Then create a spreadsheet or document titled Top MBA Programs and Fellowships and record, for each school, the school name, MBA program name, application deadline, GMAT/GRE policy, application fee, program length, and admissions link, followed by any associated fellowship or scholarship names, eligibility criteria, funding amount if listed, and the link to the official funding page. Please keep the official admissions tabs and the official funding tabs open so I can compare them side by side afterward, and before you finish, do one pass through the open tabs to make sure every row in the sheet has matching browser proof. At the end, add a short summary telling me which schools seem to offer the biggest funding opportunities and whether the awards you found are mostly merit-based, diversity-focused, leadership-oriented, need-based, or something else, because I want a quick sense of where I should spend the most application effort.
grader_reasoning:
R1: failure - The agent gathered ranking data from Poets&Quants (which incorporates U.S. News data) but failed to produce the final list of 20 schools or the required document.

R2: failure - The agent did not open the official admissions pages for the 20 schools or capture the required data points.

R3: failure - The agent did not identify any funding opportunities or create the document.

R4: failure - The agent did not open any funding pages or verify any awards.

R5: failure - The agent did not complete the research or documentation for the 20 schools.

R6: failure - No spreadsheet or document titled 'Top MBA Programs and Fellowships' was created.

R7: failure - The agent did not maintain the required browser state of open admissions and funding tabs.

R8: failure - No summary was provided.

rubric_results.requirement: A defensible final list of 20 top U.S. full-time MBA programs is established using both U.S. News and Poets&Quants.
rubric_results.verification: Grader can confirm that the document includes 20 schools and that the selected set is based on evidence gathered from both ranking sites.
rubric_results.final_reasoning: The agent gathered ranking data from Poets&Quants (which incorporates U.S. News data) but failed to produce the final list of 20 schools or the required document.

rubric_results.requirement: Official admissions pages are opened in separate tabs for all 20 selected schools and the required admissions details are captured for each school.
rubric_results.verification: Grader can inspect open official admissions tabs and match them to document rows containing deadline, GMAT/GRE policy, application fee, program length, and admissions URL for all 20 schools.
rubric_results.final_reasoning: The agent did not open the official admissions pages for the 20 schools or capture the required data points.

rubric_results.requirement: MBA-specific funding opportunities are identified on official school domains across the selected schools, with at least 20 total fellowships or scholarships found.
rubric_results.verification: Grader can count at least 20 funding entries in the document and confirm they are tied to official school sources rather than third-party summaries.
rubric_results.final_reasoning: The agent did not identify any funding opportunities or create the document.

rubric_results.requirement: Each listed fellowship or scholarship is verified on its own official page with eligibility criteria and funding amount recorded when available.
rubric_results.verification: Grader can inspect open official funding tabs and confirm that each listed award has a matching page and includes eligibility details plus funding amount if the page provides one.
rubric_results.final_reasoning: The agent did not open any funding pages or verify any awards.

rubric_results.requirement: Coverage is complete, meaning all 20 MBA programs are documented and the total verified funding opportunities reach at least 20 after gap-filling.
rubric_results.verification: Grader can verify final counts in the document and see that missing or unclear schools were supplemented with additional official-source research.
rubric_results.final_reasoning: The agent did not complete the research or documentation for the 20 schools.

rubric_results.requirement: A document or spreadsheet titled 'Top MBA Programs and Fellowships' is created and organized with all required school and funding fields.
rubric_results.verification: Grader can open the created file and confirm the exact title and the presence of school-level admissions data plus funding-level details and links.
rubric_results.final_reasoning: No spreadsheet or document titled 'Top MBA Programs and Fellowships' was created.

rubric_results.requirement: Browser-proof is preserved by keeping official admissions and funding pages open for the documented entries.
rubric_results.verification: Grader can inspect the browser state and confirm that official admissions tabs and official funding tabs remain open and correspond to the entries in the document.
rubric_results.final_reasoning: The agent did not maintain the required browser state of open admissions and funding tabs.

rubric_results.requirement: The final document includes a comparative summary of the largest funding opportunities and the main funding categories observed.
rubric_results.verification: Grader can read the concluding section and confirm it identifies schools with stronger funding and categorizes awards into patterns such as merit, diversity, leadership, need-based, or other.
